name: Torrens Island BESS
notable: 250 MW / 250 MWh BESS at AGL's Torrens Island gas plant site in SA. Wärtsilä-supplied system completed in 18 months, designed for Virtual Synchronous Generator (VSG) grid-forming operation — the largest BESS globally capable of VSG at time of commissioning.

timeline.title: Commercial operations commenced
timeline.detail: Commencement date from AEMO registration via OpenElectricity.

timeline.title: First generation recorded
timeline.detail: First market dispatch data recorded in AEMO systems.

timeline.title: Commercial operations begin
timeline.detail: AGL's 250MW/250MWh Torrens Island BESS reached COD after 18-month build by Wartsila, becoming SA's largest battery at the time.

timeline.title: First AEMO minimum system load direction
timeline.detail: AEMO directed Torrens Island BESS to charge under minimum system load protocols — first time a BESS was subject to MSL directions in the NEM.

timeline.title: Second consecutive AEMO MSL direction
timeline.detail: AEMO directed the battery for a second consecutive day, preventing charging during optimal pricing windows.

timeline.title: Third AEMO MSL direction in one week
timeline.detail: Third MSL direction issued within same week, establishing pattern of grid-security interventions overriding battery economics.

eis_specs.document_title: Torrens Island BESS Development Application (SA EPA)
eis_specs.connection_substation_name: Torrens Island 275/66 kV substation (former power station, on-site)
eis_specs.connection_distance_note: On-site — at the former Torrens Island Power Station site, Port River, near Adelaide CBD.
eis_specs.connection_augmentation: Minimal — former 480 MW gas plant infrastructure reused. ElectraNet Torrens Island 275/66 kV substation retained and adapted. Strategic location close to Adelaide CBD load centre.
eis_specs.notes: 250 MW / 250 MWh (1-hour) BESS at former Torrens Island Power Station, Port River, Adelaide SA. Developer: AGL Energy. Technology: Wartsila Quantum (CATL LFP cells). Inverter: SMA Sunny Central Storage 3600 UP-XT. EPC: Wartsila. SA development approval 2021. Now operating. Excellent brownfield grid connection at 275 kV. Strategic SA firming near Adelaide.
